              </td>
              <td align="right" valign="bottom" width="23%">
                <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">7.0496</font></div>
              </td>
              <td align="right" valign="bottom" width="23%">
                <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">6.3399</font></div>
              </td>
            </tr>
        </table>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 18pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Source:
Pacific Exchange Rate Service</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-STYLE: italic">Comprehensive
Income</font> &#8211; Comprehensive income includes the effect of fluctuations in
foreign currency rates on the values of our foreign investments and fair value
gains and losses on the interest rate swap agreements we maintain from time to
time to hedge against interest rate increases on our credit
facilities.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-STYLE: italic">Operating
Segment</font> &#8211; Beginning in the fourth quarter 2007, the Company modified its
segment reporting from seven reportable segments to one reportable segment, as
the Company now believes that its properties can be aggregated together in
accordance with SFAS 131, &#8220;Disclosures about Segments of an Enterprise and
Related Information.&#8221; Based on a review of SFAS 131, the Company has determined
that it operates primarily in one segment, the operation of casino facilities,
which includes the provision of gaming, hotel accommodations, dining facilities
and other amenities. As a gaming company, the Company&#8217;s operating results are
highly dependent on the volume of customers at our casinos. Most of the
Company&#8217;s revenue is essentially cash-based, through customers wagering with
cash or paying for non-gaming services with cash or credit cards.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div id="PGBRK" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div id="FTR">
        <div id="GLFTR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="left">
        </div>
      </div>
      <div id="PN" style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always">
        <div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">-F12-</font></div>
        <div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center">
          <hr style="COLOR: black" noshade size="2">
        </div>
      </div>
      <div id="HDR">
        <div id="GLHDR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="right">
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-STYLE: italic">Revenue
Recognition</font> - Casino revenue is the net win from gaming activities, which
is the difference between gaming wins and losses. Hotel, food and beverage
revenues are recognized when products are delivered or services are performed.
Management and consulting fees are recognized as revenue when services are
provided.<font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt">&#160;</font>The
incremental amount of unpaid progressive jackpots is recorded as a liability and
a reduction of casino revenue in the period during which the progressive jackpot
increases.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Revenues
are recognized net of certain sales incentives in accordance with the Emerging
Issues Task Force&#160;&#160;(&#8220;EITF&#8221;) consensus on Issue 01-9, &#8220;Accounting for
Consideration Given by a Vendor to a Customer (Including a Reseller of the
Vendor&#8217;s products)&#8221;. EITF 01-9 requires that sales incentives and points earned
in point-loyalty programs be recorded as a reduction of revenue. The Company
recognizes incentives related to gaming play and points earned in point-loyalty
programs as a direct reduction of revenues.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The
retail value of accommodations, food and beverage, and other services furnished
to guests without charge is included in gross revenues and then deducted as
promotional allowances. The estimated cost of providing such promotional
allowances is primarily included in hotel, food and beverage
expenses.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">We issue
free play or coupons for the purpose of generating future revenue. Coupons are
issued the month prior to when they can be redeemed and are valid for defined
periods of time ranging up to 7 days. The net win from the coupons is expected
to exceed the value of the coupons issued. The cost of the coupons redeemed is
applied against the revenue generated on the day of the redemption.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Members
of our casinos&#8217; player&#8217;s clubs earn points based on their volume of play
(typically as a percentage of coin-in) at certain of our casinos. Players can
accumulate points over time that they may redeem at their discretion under the
terms of the program. Points can be redeemed for cash and/or various amenities
at the casino, such as meals, hotel stays and gift shop items. The cost of the
points is offset against the revenue generated in the period during which the
points were earned. The value of unused or unredeemed points is included in
accounts payable and accrued liabilities on our consolidated balance sheet. The
expiration of unused points results in a reduction of the corresponding
liability.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Promotional
allowances presented in the consolidated statement of earnings for 2007, 2006
and 2005 include the following:</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-STYLE: italic">Stock-Based
Compensation</font> &#8211; Prior to January 1, 2006, the Company accounted for stock
options under the recognition and measurement provisions of APB Opinion No. 25,
&#8220;Accounting for Stock Issued to Employees,&#8221; and related interpretations.
Effective January 1, 2006, the Company adopted the provisions of SFAS 123
(Revised 2004), &#8220;Share-Based Payment&#8221; (&#8220;SFAS 123R&#8221;), and selected the modified
prospective method to initially report stock-based compensation amounts in the
consolidated financial statements. Under the provisions of SFAS 123R,
stock-based compensation cost is measured at the grant date based on the fair
value of the award and is recognized as expense over the vesting period. The
Company is currently using the Black-Scholes option pricing model to determine
the fair value of all option grants.</font></div>

    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-STYLE: italic">Advertising
Costs</font> &#8211; The Company expenses advertising costs when incurred. Advertising
expense was $2.3 million, $1.6 million and $0.7 million for the years ended
December 31, 2007, 2006 and 2005, respectively.</font></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-STYLE: italic">Preopening and
Start-Up Expenses </font>&#8211; The Company accounts for costs incurred during the
preopening and start-up phases of operations in accordance with Statement of
Position 98-5, &#8220;Reporting on the Costs of Start-Up Activities.&#8221; Preopening and
start-up costs, including organizational costs, are expensed as incurred. There
were no preopening expenses in 2007. The Company incurred approximately $4.0
million of preopening expenses for the year ended December 31, 2006 towards its
projects in Central City, Colorado, Edmonton, Canada, and Newcastle, South
Africa. The Company incurred approximately $0.4 million of preopening expenses
in 2005 towards its project in Central City, Colorado. By agreement, the entire
loss was allocated to the minority partner in this project.</font></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-STYLE: italic">Income
Taxes</font> - The Company accounts for income taxes using the asset and
liability method, which provides that deferred tax assets and liabilities are
recorded based on the difference between the tax bases of assets and liabilities
and their carrying amounts for financial reporting purposes, at a rate expected
to be in effect when the differences become deductible or payable.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-STYLE: italic">Earnings Per
Share </font>&#8211; Basic earnings per share considers only weighted-average
outstanding common shares in the computation. Diluted earnings per share gives
effect to all potentially dilutive securities. Diluted earnings per share is
based upon the weighted average number of common shares outstanding during the
period, plus, if dilutive, the assumed exercise of stock options using the
treasury stock method and the assumed conversion of other convertible securities
(using the &#8220;if converted&#8221; method) at the beginning of the year, or for the
period outstanding during the year for current year
issuances.</font></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-STYLE: italic">Recent Accounting
Pronouncements</font> &#8211;<font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;</font>In
December 2007, the FASB issued SFAS 141 (revised 2007), &#8220;Business Combinations&#8221;
(&#8220;SFAS 141R&#8221;), which replaces SFAS 141. SFAS 141R establishes principles and
requirements regarding how an acquirer recognizes and measures in its financial
statements the identifiable assets acquired, the liabilities assumed, any
noncontrolling interest in the acquiree and the goodwill acquired. SFAS 141R
also establishes disclosure requirements that will enable users to evaluate the
nature and financial effects of the business combination. SFAS 141R is effective
for acquisitions made in fiscal years beginning after December 15, 2008. The
Company is currently evaluating the potential impact, if any, of the adoption of
SFAS 141R on its consolidated financial statements.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">In
December 2007, the FASB issued SFAS 160, &#8220;Noncontrolling Interests in
Consolidated Financial Statements&#8212;an amendment of ARB No. 51&#8221; (&#8220;SFAS 160&#8221;). SFAS
160 requires that accounting and reporting for minority interests will be
recharacterized as noncontrolling interests and classified as a component of
equity. SFAS 160 also establishes reporting requirements that provide sufficient
disclosures that clearly identify and distinguish between the interests of the
parent and the interests of the noncontrolling owners. SFAS 160 is effective for
fiscal years beginning after December 15, 2008. Based upon the December 31, 2007
balance sheet, the impact of adopting SFAS 160 would be to reclassify $5.8
million in minority interests in consolidated subsidiaries from total
liabilities to a separate component of shareholders&#8217; equity.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt">&#160;</div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt">&#160;</div>
    <div id="PGBRK" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">

    <div>
      <table align="center" border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" id="hangingindent" width="100%">
          <tr valign="top">
            <td style="WIDTH: 36pt">
              <div><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;
      </font></div>
            </td>
            <td>
              <div align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">In
      February 2007, the FASB issued SFAS 159, &#8220;The Fair Value Option for
      Financial Assets and Financial Liabilities&#8221; (&#8220;SFAS 159&#8221;), which permits an
      entity to measure certain financial assets and financial liabilities at
      fair value. SFAS 159 is effective for fiscal years beginning January 1,
      2008. The Company does not expect this statement to have a material impact
      on the Company&#8217;s results of operations or financial position upon
      adoption.</font></div>
            </td>
          </tr>
      </table>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">In
September 2006, the FASB issued SFAS 157, &#8220;Fair Value Measurements&#8221; (&#8220;SFAS
157&#8221;). SFAS 157 defines fair value, establishes a framework for measuring fair
value and expands disclosure regarding fair value measurements. This statement
applies to other accounting pronouncements that require or permit fair value
measurements for which the FASB has previously concluded in those announcements
that fair value is the relevant measurement attribute. Accordingly, this
statement does not require any new fair value measurements. The effective date
of SFAS 157 is for fiscal years beginning after November 15, 2007, with the
ability to defer the adoption of the provisions pertaining to nonfinancial
assets and liabilities for one year. The Company does not expect this statement
to have a material impact on the Company&#8217;s results of operations or financial
position upon adoption.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On

    <div id="PGBRK" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div id="FTR">
        <div style="WIDTH: 100%" align="left">
          <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt">&#160;</div>
        </div>
        <div id="GLFTR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 8pt; COLOR: #000000; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">&#160;
</font></div>

      <div id="PN" style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always">
        <div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">-F15-</font></div>

      </div>
    </div><br>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; TEXT-DECORATION: underline">Century Casino
Newcastle</font></font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On April
1, 2006, our subsidiary CCA completed the purchase of a 60% controlling interest
in Balele, the owner of a casino in Newcastle, South Africa, for approximately
$9.3 million (ZAR 57.5 million). Following the purchase, we began the
development of a new casino in Newcastle.&#160;In connection with CCA&#8217;s purchase
of its equity interest in Balele, a sale of share agreement (the &#8220;Share
Agreement&#8221;) was entered into on October 18, 2005 between CCA and a group of
Balele shareholders (the &#8220;Sellers&#8221;).&#160;As a condition to the Share Agreement,
the Sellers provided a warranty to CCA that the Sellers would be able to sell
the facility that housed the original casino for approximately $1.9 million (ZAR
12.0 million) within 60 days of closing.&#160;The Sellers informed CCA that they
would not be able to sell the facility for ZAR 12.0 million.&#160;As a result,
the purchase price for the 60% interest in Balele was reduced by this amount,
resulting in an overall purchase price of $7.5 million (ZAR 45.5
million).&#160;We paid $6.7 million (ZAR 40.5 million) at closing. The following
table summarizes the fair values of the assets acquired and liabilities assumed
at the date of acquisition:</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On
December 14, 2007, CCA paid an additional $0.6 million (ZAR 3.7 million) towards
the purchase. The remaining $0.2 million (ZAR 1.3 million) is payable subject to
the finalization of a South Africa Revenue Service tax audit pertaining to
periods prior to the Company&#8217;s acquisition of its 60.0% interest. The Company
consolidated the results of Balele, now known as Century Casino Newcastle (Pty)
Ltd. (&#8220;CNEW&#8221;), in its financial statements as of April 1, 2006.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; TEXT-DECORATION: underline">Century Casino
Millennium</font></font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Prior to
April 13, 2006, CCE had a 50% equity ownership in CM. On April 13, 2006, CCE
purchased the remaining 50% of CM for approximately $0.7 million, which included
a security deposit for CM&#8217;s casino license in the amount of $0.4 million (CZK
10.0 million), the assumption of loans previously granted by the former partner
and the purchase price for the former partner&#8217;s 50% equity
interest.</font></div>

13, 2006, CCE entered into an agreement to acquire 100% of all of the issued and
outstanding shares of G5, a Polish entity that owns a 33.3% interest in CPL, for
approximately $3.8 million (&#8364;2.9 million). In connection with the purchase, CCE
loaned G5 approximately $4.8 million (PLN 15.0 million) to repay existing loans
between G5 and its creditors. On February 1, 2007, CCE loaned G5 an additional
$1.0 million (PLN 3.0 million) to repay additional amounts to creditors. As a
result of the loan, the purchase price of the shares was reduced by $1.0 million
(&#8364;0.7 million).</font></div>

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On
December 31, 2007, CTI acquired from Central City Venture LLC (&#8220;Venture&#8221;) the
remaining 35% of all issued and outstanding partnership units of CTL that CTL
did not already own. CTL owns and operates the Century Casino &amp; Hotel, a
limited-stakes gaming facility, in Central City, Colorado. The purchase price
for the 35% equity interest was $2.1 million. In addition, CTI purchased a $1.0
million note previously issued to Venture by CTL and paid $0.2 million of
accrued interest on the note. The Company is in the process of finalizing its
purchase accounting for this transaction. The following table summarizes the
estimated fair values of the assets acquired and liabilities assumed at the date
of acquisition:</font></div>

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">These

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">4.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;EQUITY
INVESTMENT IN UNCONSOLIDATED SUBSIDIARY</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Prior to
April 13, 2006, we had a 50% ownership in CM and we accounted for this
investment under the equity method. For the year ended December 31, 2005, CM had
net operating revenues of $2.1 million and a net loss of $0.4 million. The
Company only recorded its portion of CM&#8217;s losses that reduced its net investment
to zero.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">As
indicated in Note 3 above, the Company acquired a 33.3% ownership interest in
CPL on March 12, 2007. We account for this investment under the equity
method.</font></div>

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">During
2007, in accordance with EITF 93-7, &#8220;Uncertainties Related to Income Taxes in a
Purchase Business Combination&#8221; (&#8220;EITF 93-7&#8221;), the Company adjusted the
allocation of CCAL&#8217;s purchase price resulting from the derecognition of a
deferred tax asset established at the time of acquisition. The adjustment
decreased deferred income taxes &#8211; foreign by $0.1 million and increased goodwill
by $0.1 million.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Changes
in the carrying amount of intangible assets not subject to amortization, which
includes casino licenses, for the years ended December 31, 2007 and 2006 are as
follows:</font></div>

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Revolving
Credit Facility &#8211; Cripple Creek</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">On April </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">21</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">, 2000, the Company and Wells
Fargo Bank (the &#8220;Bank&#8221;) entered into an Amended and Restated Credit
Agreement</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">, which
has since been further amended</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> (the &#8220;</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Amended Womacks </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Agreement&#8221;)</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">.</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">&#160;</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">The Amended Womacks Agreement
provided the Company with an original aggregate</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> borrowing commitment from
the Bank under a Revolving Line of Credit Facility (&#8220;</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Womacks </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">RCF&#8221;) </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">of</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> $26 million</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">.</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">&#160;</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Based on WMCK&#8217;s maximum
leverage ratio (as defined in the Amended Womacks Agreement), there is no
remaining availability on the Womacks RCF as of December 31, 2007</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">.</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> The Amended Womacks
Agreement is guaranteed by the Company and permits the Company to enter into a
management agreement with WMCK, subject to certain limitations. The Amended
Womacks Agreement permitted the Company to make capital contributions to Womacks
for a specified period of time for the repayment of the outstanding obligations
under the Womacks RCF, and subsequently permits Womacks to make cash
distributions to the Company up to the amount of the Company&#8217;s capital
contributions (maximum of $7.9 million as of December 31, 2007, subject to
covenant limitations) made during the specified period. </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Interest on the </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Amended Womacks </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Agreement is variable based
on the</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> LIBOR or
prime</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> interest
rate option selected by the Company, plus an applicable margin based on
</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">WMCK</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">&#8217;s</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> consolidated</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> leverage ratio.</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">&#160;</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">The </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Amended Womacks </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Agreement also requires a
nonusage fee based on </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">WMCK&#8217;s
consolidated</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">
leverage ratio on the unused portion of the commitment.</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">&#160;</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">The principal balance
outstanding under the </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Amended Womacks
Agreement</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> as of
December 31,</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">
2007 and</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">
200</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">6</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> was </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">$10.8 million and $7.1
million</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">,
respectively.</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">&#160;</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">On March 14, 2008, the
maturity date was extended to December 31, 2009 and the </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">aggregate commitment
available to the Company </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">was reduced to $10.0 million
by agreement</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">.</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> As of March 14, 2008, the
principal balance outstanding under the Amended Womacks Agreement was $7.4
million.</font></font></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">The</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> Amended Womacks
Agreement</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">
includes certain restrictive covenants on financial ratios of WMCK.</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">&#160;</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">The most significant
covenants include i) a maximum leverage ratio no greater than </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">2.00 to 1.00</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">, ii) a minimum interest
coverage ratio no less than 2.00 to 1.00 </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">and iii) a minimum quarterly
EBITDA requirement</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">. </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">In the event that WMCK does
not comply with the minimum quarterly EBITDA requirement, the Company will have
the ability to make a cash payment to reduce the principal balance outstanding
under the Amended Womacks Agreement. This additional payment will be considered
EBITDA for purposes of calculating the minimum quarterly EBITDA requirement.
</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">The Company is
in compliance with the restrictive covenants on the financial ratios of WMCK
contained in the </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Womacks </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">RCF as of December
31,</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> 2007
and</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">
200</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">6</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">. </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">In addition to the Company&#8217;s
guarantee, t</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">he
loan is collateralized by a deed of trust and a security agreement with
assignments of lease, rents and furniture, fixtures and equipment of
</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Womacks</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> property.</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> The interest rate at
December 31, 2007 was 7</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">.25</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">% for the balance of the loan
outstanding under the prime based provisions of the Amended Womacks
Agreement.</font></font></div>

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">In May
2000, we entered into a five-year interest rate swap agreement on $4.0 million
notional amount of debt under the Womacks RCF, whereby we paid a LIBOR-based
fixed rate of 7.95% and received a LIBOR-based floating rate reset quarterly
based on a three-month rate. This swap agreement expired on July 1, 2005. We
have not entered into any new swap agreements as of December 31, 2007. Our
objective for entering into the interest rate swap agreement, a derivative
instrument designated as a cash flow hedging instrument, was to eliminate the
variability of cash flows in the interest payments for a portion of the Womacks
RCF. Net additional interest expense to us under the swap agreement was $0.1
million in 2005. Including the impact of the swap and the amortization of the
deferred financing cost, the effective rate on the borrowings under the Womacks
RCF was 9.0%, 10.0% and 7.3% for 2007, 2006 and 2005, respectively.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Term
Loan and Revolving Credit Facility &#8211; Central City</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On
November 21, 2005, CTL entered into a $35 million loan agreement, which has
since been amended (&#8220;Amended Tollgate Agreement&#8221;), with Wells Fargo Bank and a
syndicate of institutional lenders. The Amended Tollgate Agreement consists of a
$32.5 million construction loan and a $2.5 million revolving line of credit (&#8220;CC
Revolver&#8221;). The $32.5 million construction loan converted to a 60-month term
loan (&#8220;CC Term Loan&#8221;) on November 22, 2006 (&#8220;Conversion Date&#8221;). The CC Term Loan
and the CC Revolver will both mature on November 22, 2011. Effective December
31, 2007, the amount outstanding under the CC Term Loan is subject to quarterly
reductions of $0.6 million. Availability under the CC Revolver is conditional
upon CTL being in compliance with all of the financial and other covenants
contained in the loan agreement at the time of a particular drawdown, and the
Company&#8217;s continued ability to satisfy certain representations and warranties,
including representations as to the absence of liens on the Central City
properties other than certain permitted liens, the absence of litigation or
other developments that have or could reasonably be expected to have a material
adverse effect on the Company and its subsidiaries, and continued effectiveness
of the documents granting security for the credit facility. The interest rate on
both loans, as amended, is the greater of 6.5% or the Prime Rate plus 2.0%
(9.25% as of December 31, 2007) and a service fee of 0.5% on the total
outstanding balance, payable monthly. Upon closing, CTL incurred a facility fee
of $1.1 million payable to Wells Fargo Bank which was satisfied from the
proceeds of the loan. The Amended Tollgate Agreement is subject to a prepayment
fee, currently at 7.6%, which decreases annually to 2.3% in the 17<font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 70%; VERTICAL-ALIGN: super">th</font> full
quarter after the Conversion Date through maturity; a nonusage fee of 0.75% on
the unused portion of the total commitment; various reporting requirements and
various financial covenants, the most significant being Senior Leverage Ratio,
Adjusted Fixed Charge Coverage, Limitation on Indebtedness and Restriction on
Distributions.<font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">&#160;</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">The Company is in compliance
with the restrictive covenants on the financial ratios of </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">CTL</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> contained in the
</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Amended Tollgate
Agreement</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> as of
December 31,</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">
2007 and 2006</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">.
</font>The Amended Tollgate Agreement is secured by all of CTL&#8217;s assets and is
guaranteed by the Company. <font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">The principal balance
outstanding under the </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Amended Tollgate
Agreement</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> as of
December 31, 200</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">7 was $19.8 million,
</font>of which $17.4 million is considered long-term in the accompanying
December 31, 2007 consolidated balance sheet. During 2007, the Company made
early repayments of $12.1 million of principal towards the CC Term Loan. CTL
incurred prepayment charges of $0.3 million relating to these early repayments.
The prepayment charges have been classified as a component of long-term deferred
financing costs on the December 31, 2007 consolidated balance sheet. These
charges will be payable on the maturity of the Amended Tollgate
Agreement.</font></div>

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The
Company believes that it is probable that CTL will not be in compliance with a
financial covenant for the quarter ended March 31, 2008. Prior to March 31,
2008, CTL plans to seek a waiver from Wells Fargo related to this covenant or
possibly seek an amendment to the Amended Tollgate Agreement. There can be no
assurance that Wells Fargo will grant any waiver or agree to any amendment that
might be necessary. If CTL fails to meet any of its debt covenants and Wells
Fargo does not grant a waiver or agree to amend the facility, the lenders would
have the right to declare an event of default and seek remedies, including the
acceleration of all outstanding amounts due under the Amended Tollgate Agreement
or a call for CCI to repay the loan pursuant to its guarantee. </font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Term Loan &#8211; </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Newcastle</font></font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">On December 7, 2006, CNEW
received a $7.1 million (ZAR 50.0 million) term loan from Nedbank Limited
(&#8220;Nedbank&#8221;), the proceeds of which were used to fund the construction of a new
casino and hotel in </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Newcastle</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">, </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">South Africa</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">. Pursuant to the loan
agreement, monthly principal and interest payments of approximately $0.2 million
(ZAR </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">1.1
million</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">) are due
on the first day of each month. The loan matures on December 7, 2011.
</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">The </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">term loan</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> bears interest at South
Africa&#8217;s prime interest rate less 1.5% (which equates to </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">13.0</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">%</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> as of December 31,
2007</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">) and is
secured by the pledge of 100% of the total outstanding common stock of
</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">CNEW</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">.</font> As of December 31,
2007, $6.0 million of principal is outstanding, of which $4.8 million is
considered long-term in the accompanying December 31, 2007 consolidated balance
sheet.</font></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Term
Loan &#8211; Caledon</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On August
26, 2005, CCAL received a $9.4 million (ZAR 60.0 million) term loan from
Nedbank, the net proceeds of which were used for the repayment of existing bank
debt and additional working capital needs. In connection with the loan, CCAL and
Nedbank entered into a loan agreement specifying the terms of the loan. Pursuant
to the loan agreement, monthly principal and interest installment payments of
$0.2 million (ZAR 1.3 million) are due on the first day of each month. The loan
matures on September 1, 2010, at which time any outstanding principal and
interest will be due. The term loan bears interest at South Africa&#8217;s prime
interest rate less 1.5% (which equates to 13.0% as of December 31, 2007) and is
secured by $1.5 million (ZAR 10.0 million) of CCAL&#8217;s assets and 100% of the
issued share capital of CCAL. CCAL has the option to prepay the loan, without
penalty, upon 90 days written notice. As of December 31, 2007, $5.4 million of
principal is outstanding, of which $3.6 million is considered long-term in the
accompanying December 31, 2007 consolidated balance sheet.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Term
Loan - Edmonton</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On
September 23, 2005, CRA agreed to the terms of a $20.1 million (CAD 20.0
million) credit facility with Canadian Western Bank (&#8220;CWB&#8221;) for the development
of the Century Casino &amp; Hotel in Edmonton, Alberta, Canada. The credit
facility, originally structured as a construction loan, converted to a 60-month
mortgage (&#8220;Mortgage&#8221;) on December 20, 2007. The Mortgage matures on December 31,
2012. Prior to December 31, 2012, CRA will be able to renegotiate the Mortgage
for an additional one to five year term. The Mortgage bears interest at 7.0%.
Monthly principal and interest payments on the Mortgage are based on a 10-year
amortization and are payable on the last day of each month. Under the terms of
the Mortgage, CRA is subject to various reporting requirements, a minimum equity
requirement and is required to maintain a minimum Cash Flow Coverage Ratio
(<font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">as defined in the
</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Mortgage) of
1.20.</font> The Mortgage is secured by the assets of CRA and guaranteed by the
Company. CRA may elect to prepay up to 10%, or $2.0 million (CAD 2.0 million),
of the original principal amount of the Mortgage annually without penalty or
bonus; prepayment of any additional amounts is subject to three months interest
at the fixed interest rate. <font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">The principal balance
outstanding under the </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Mortgage </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">as of December 31,
200</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">7</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> was </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">$20.1 millio</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">n</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">, of which $18.7 million is
considered</font> long-term in the accompanying December 31, 2007 consolidated
balance sheet.</font></div>

      <div id="PN" style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always">

    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The
Company has two non-interest bearing stand-by letter of credit agreements
totaling $0.1 million with CWB. These letters of credit have no maturity date
and are cancelable upon written notice by CRA. CRA will incur a 1% charge for
each year the letters of credit are outstanding. These letters of credit
guarantee to the City of Edmonton, Alberta, Canada the completion of certain
landscaping requirements at the casino. As of December 31, 2007, there were
no&#160;&#160;balances outstanding under these letters of credit.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Notes
payable and other</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">As
mentioned in Note 3, CCA completed the purchase of a 60% controlling interest in
CNEW on April 1, 2006 for approximately $7.4 million (ZAR 45.5 million). The
Company has paid $7.2 million (ZAR 44.2 million) towards the purchase. The
remaining $0.2 million (ZAR 1.3 million), which is payable subject to the
finalization of a South Africa Revenue Service tax audit pertaining to periods
prior to the Company&#8217;s acquisition of its 60.0% interest, is classified as a
current liability on the December 31, 2007 consolidated balance
sheet.</font></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">In conjunction with the
purchase of CNEW, the Company agreed to reimburse the previous ownership of CNEW
</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">for all
professional fees and disbursements incurred by </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">the previous ownership
</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">relating to the
development of the </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">new casino be</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">tween the </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">date the agreements were
signed a</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">nd</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> date of c</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">losing. </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">As agreed, the</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> reimbursement </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">will</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> take place when the
directors of </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">CNEW consider</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> that the available cash
resources of </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">CNEW</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> so permit</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> and will be paid from CNEW&#8217;s
available funds</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">.</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">&#160;</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">As a result of this
agreement, the Company has a current liability of $1.5 million (ZAR 10.3
million) on the December 31, 2007 consolidated balance
sheet.</font></font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">The remaining debt at
December 31, 2007 consists primarily of capital leases at our </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Caledon</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> and </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">Edmonton</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">
properties.</font></font></div>

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">In July
2007, the Company issued 200,000 shares of restricted common stock with a fair
value of $9.00 per share to each of its Co Chief Executive Officers. The
restricted stock vests ratably over a four-year period. <font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">In October 2007</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">, the Company issued
</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">1</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">00,000 </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">new </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">shares of its common
stock</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> for stock
options exercised in cash</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> at an exercise price of
$</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">2.93</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> per share.</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">&#160;</font>In May 2006, the
Company issued 600,000 new shares of its common stock, at an exercise price of
$0.75 per share, for stock options exercised in cash.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">In March 2000, the
Company&#8217;s</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">&#160;</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">b</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">oard of </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">d</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">irectors approved a
discretionary program to repurchase up to $5.0&#160;million of the Company&#8217;s
outstanding common stock.</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> The Company</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> did not purchase any shares
of </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">its</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> common stock on the open
market in </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">2007
or </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">200</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">6</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">.</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">&#160;</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">The total remaining
authorization under the repurchase program was $1.2 million as of December 31,
200</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">7</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">.</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">&#160;</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">The repurchase program has no
set expiration or termination date.</font></font></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">There were </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">11,376</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff"> shares in treasury as of
December 31, 200</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">7</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">, at an average cost per
share of $</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">2.26</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">. </font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; BACKGROUND-COLOR: #ffffff">We re-issued 153,000 shares
of treasury stock in 2007 for stock options exercised in cash, 28,000 of which
were to satisfy directors&#8217; options. In 2006, a total of </font>23,500 shares
were re-issued to satisfy option exercises, 16,000 of which were to satisfy
directors&#8217; options.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-STYLE: italic">Subsidiary
Preference Shares -</font><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold">&#160;</font>In connection with the
granting of a gaming license to CCAL by the Western Cape Gambling and Racing
Board in April 2000, CCAL issued a total of 200 preference shares, 100 shares
each to two minority shareholders, each of whom has one seat on the board of
directors of CCAL, and neither of whom is an officer, director or affiliate of
CCI. In January 2006, 200 preference shares of a new class (&#8220;Class A shares&#8221;)
were authorized for issuance. The Class A shares are neither cumulative nor
redeemable. Each Class A share entitles the holder to dividends of 0.009% of the
annual gross gambling revenue of the Caledon Hotel, Spa &amp; Casino after the
deduction of gaming taxes and value added tax. Furthermore, if the casino
business is sold or otherwise dissolved, for each Class A share held, the
shareholder would be entitled to 0.009% of any surplus directly attributable to
the casino business, net of all liabilities attributable to the casino business.
As of December 31, 2006, one preference shareholder accepted the offer to
transfer all 100 of its original preference shares for 100 of the new Class A
shares. In March 2007, the second of the two preference shareholders accepted
the offer to transfer all 100 of its original preference shares for 100 Class A
shares. Each preference shareholder was paid ZAR 5,000 per share as an incentive
to exchange their original preference shares for Class A
shares.</font></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">CCAL paid
$0.4 million (ZAR 2.8 million) and $0.1 million (ZAR 1.0 million) of preference
dividends in 2007 and 2006, respectively. Preference dividends were not paid,
nor were they payable, in 2005.</font></div>
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify">&#160;</div>
      <div id="PGBRK" style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt">
        <div id="FTR">
          <div id="GLFTR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="left">
          </div>
        </div>
        <div id="PN" style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always">
          <div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">-F25-</font></div>
          <div style="WIDTH: 100%; TEXT-ALIGN: center">
            <hr style="COLOR: black" noshade size="2">
          </div>

        <div id="HDR">
          <div id="GLHDR" style="WIDTH: 100%" align="right">
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><br>&#160;</div>
    </div>
    <div>
      <table align="center" border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" id="hangingindent" width="100%">
          <tr valign="top">
            <td style="WIDTH: 36pt">
              <div style="MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">10.</font></div>
            </td>
            <td>
              <div align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">STOCK-BASED
      COMPENSATION</font></div>
            </td>
          </tr>
      </table>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The board
of directors of the Company adopted an Employees&#8217; Equity Incentive Plan (the
&#8220;EEIP&#8221;) in April 1994, which expired in April 2004. The EEIP continues to be
administered for previously issued and outstanding options. Stockholders of the
Company approved a new equity incentive plan (the &#8220;2005 Plan&#8221;) at the 2005
annual meeting of stockholders. The 2005 Plan provides for the grant of awards
to eligible individuals in the form of stock, restricted stock, stock options,
performance units or other stock-based awards, all as defined in the 2005 Plan.
The 2005 Plan provides for the issuance of up to 2,000,000 shares of common
stock to eligible individuals through the various forms of permitted awards. The
2005 Plan limits the number of options that can be awarded to an eligible
individual to 200,000 per year. Stock options may not be issued at an option
price lower than fair market value at the date of grant. All stock options must
have an exercise period not to exceed ten years. Through December 31, 2007, the
Company has granted under the EEIP and the 2005 Plan restricted common shares,
incentive stock option awards (for which the option price was not less than the
fair market value at the date of grant) and non-statutory options (which may be
granted at any option price (as permitted under the EEIP)). Options granted to
date have one-year, two-year or four-year vesting periods. Through December 31,
2007, all outstanding options have been issued at market value as of the date of
the grant. The Company&#8217;s Incentive Plan Committee or, in the case of the 2005
Plan, any other committee as delegated by the board of directors, has the power
and discretion to, among other things, prescribe the terms and conditions for
the exercise of, or modification of, any outstanding awards in the event of
merger, acquisition or any other form of acquisition other than a reorganization
of the Company under the United States Bankruptcy Code or liquidation of the
Company. Both plans also allow limited transferability of any non-statutory
stock options to legal entities that are 100% owned or controlled by the
optionee or to the optionee&#8217;s family trust.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; TEXT-DECORATION: underline">Stock
Options</font></font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">As of
December 31, 2007, there were 1,161,210 options outstanding to employees of the
Company, of which 1,108,710 options were issued under the EEIP and 52,500
options were issued under the 2005 Plan.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div><br>

            <td valign="bottom" width="10%"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">&#160;
      </font></td>
            <td valign="bottom" width="2%"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">&#160;
      </font></td>
            <td align="right" valign="bottom" width="9%">
              <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">$&#160;&#160;&#160;3.5</font></div>
            </td>
            <td valign="bottom" width="10%"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">&#160;
      </font></td>
            <td valign="bottom" width="2%"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">&#160;
      </font></td>
            <td align="right" valign="bottom" width="11%">
              <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="right"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">$&#160;&#160;&#160;5.7</font></div>
            </td>
            <td valign="bottom" width="10%"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">&#160;
      </font></td>
          </tr>
      </table>
    </div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The
aggregate intrinsic value represents the difference between the Company&#8217;s
closing stock price of $6.44, $11.16 and $8.60 as of the last trading day of
2007, 2006 and 2005, respectively, and the exercise price multiplied by the
number of options outstanding or exercisable as of that date.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The
weighted-average contractual life for options outstanding to employees at
December 31, 2007 is 6.1 years.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Additionally,
25,000 options with an exercise price of $9.00 per share were issued under the
2005 Plan to independent directors of the Company in 2007. As of December 31,
2007, there were 61,000 options outstanding to independent directors of the
Company with a weighted average exercise price of $5.61.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">For the
years ended December 31, 2007 and 2006, the Company recorded $0.2 million and
$0.4 million, respectively, for stock-based compensation expense related to
stock option grants made in prior years that vested during the year. This amount
is included in general and administrative expense. The impact to both basic and
diluted earnings per share in 2007 and 2006 was $0.01. There was no capitalized
stock-based compensation expense.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">At
December 31, 2007, there was $0.2 million of total unrecognized compensation
expense related to unvested stock options remaining to be recognized. Of this
amount, $0.1 million will be recognized during 2008, and $0.1 million will be
recognized in subsequent years through 2011.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Prior to
the adoption of SFAS 123R, the Company presented all tax benefits resulting from
the exercise of stock options as operating cash flows in the consolidated
statement of cash flows. SFAS 123R requires that cash flows from the exercise of
stock options resulting from tax benefits in excess of recognized cumulative
compensation cost (excess tax benefits) be classified as financing cash flows.
For the years ended December 31, 2007 and 2006, $0.2 million and $0.4 million,
respectively, of such excess tax benefits were classified as financing cash
flows.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">In
accordance with the modified prospective transition method, the Company&#8217;s
financial statements for prior periods have not been restated to reflect
compensation expense associated with recognizing stock-based compensation under
the fair value method. The Company did not record stock-based compensation
expense related to employee stock options during 2005; however, pro forma
stock-based compensation expense for 2005 was as follows:</font></div>

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; TEXT-DECORATION: underline">Restricted

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">In 2007,
the Company issued 200,000 shares of restricted common stock with a fair value
of $9.00 per share to each of its Co Chief Executive Officers. The restricted
stock vests ratably over a four-year period. For the year ended December 31,
2007, compensation expense related to restricted stock awards totaled $0.7
million. At December 31, 2007, there was $2.9 million of total unrecognized
compensation expense related to unvested restricted stock remaining to be
recognized. Of this amount, $1.3 million will be recognized in 2008 and $1.6
million will be recognized in subsequent years through 2011.</font></div>

            <td align="right" valign="bottom" width="14%" style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 4px double"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">(61</font></td>
            <td align="left" nowrap valign="bottom" width="1%" style="PADDING-BOTTOM: 4px"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">)</font></td>

    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Recoveries
of receivables and advances</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">On
September 28, 2006, the Company sold its interest in a casino development
project located in Gauteng, South Africa for $5.3 million (ZAR 40.3 million),
less commissions of $0.1 million (ZAR 1.3 million). The Company has recorded
$5.2 million (ZAR 39.0 million) as a gain on sale of Guateng interest in the
December 31, 2006 consolidated statement of earnings. In addition, as a part of
the transaction, the Company also received $0.4 million (ZAR 3.0 million)
related to the recovery of a previously written off loan. The Company has
recorded $0.4 million (ZAR 3.0 million) as a recovery to impairments and other
write-offs in the December 31, 2006 consolidated statement of
earnings.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Write-down
of property held for sale</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">In 2005,
the Company wrote down non-operating casino property and land held for sale
located in Wells, Nevada to its appraised value less estimated costs to sell. In
2006, the Company wrote off the remaining balance of $0.4 million.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Goodwill
impairment</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 36pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="justify"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">The
Company performs annual tests of its goodwill and indefinite lived intangible
assets in accordance with SFAS 142. As a result of its 2006 testing, CCI
determined that the goodwill at CM was impaired. The value of CM was determined
using the present value of future cash flows, which depends on a number of
significant estimates including long-term revenue growth, CM&#8217;s ability to manage
expenses, expected operating margins and the discount rate used to calculate the
present value of cash flows.</font></div>
    <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt">&#160;</div>

      <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman">Deferred
income taxes reflect the net tax effects of temporary differences between the
carrying amounts of assets and liabilities for financial reporting purposes and
the amounts used for income tax purposes. The Company&#8217;s deferred incomes taxes
at December 31, 2007 and 2006 are summarized as follows:</font></div>
      <div style="DISPLAY: block; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt"><br></div>
      <div align="left">
        <table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" width="75%">
            <tr bgcolor="#cceeff">
              <td align="left" valign="bottom" width="66%" style="PADDING-BOTTOM: 2px">
                <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">Amounts
      in thousands</font></div>
                <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="left"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">Deferred
      tax assets (liabilities) &#8211; U.S. federal and state:</font></div>
              </td>
              <td valign="bottom" width="1%" style="PADDING-BOTTOM: 2px"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">&#160;</font></td>
              <td colspan="2" valign="bottom" width="15%" style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 2px solid">
                <div style="DISPLAY: block; MARGIN-LEFT: 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0pt; MARGIN-RIGHT: 0pt" align="center"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman"><font style="DISPLAY: inline">2007</font></font></div>
              </td>
              <td align="left" nowrap valign="bottom" width="1%" style="PADDING-BOTTOM: 2px"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">&#160;</font></td>
              <td valign="bottom" width="1%" style="PADDING-BOTTOM: 2px"><font style="DISPLAY: inline; FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: times new roman">&#160;</font></td>
              <td colspan="2" valign="bottom" width="15%" style="BORDER-BOTTOM: black 2px solid">
